description: Length-matched V2: Reducing immunogenicity of gene therapies and biologics - vector immunity, protein engineering, and immune modulation.

facts.context: Pre-existing neutralizing antibodies (NAbs) against AAV vectors affect therapy by:
facts.truth: preventing vector transduction, requiring NAb screening before dosing
distractors: enhancing receptor-mediated endocytosis, improving nuclear entry of transgenes
opsonizing capsids for macrophage uptake, boosting antigen presentation
only affecting second or later doses, with first administration fully unimpaired

facts.context: Engineered AAV capsids can evade immunity by:
facts.truth: modifying surface epitopes while preserving transduction (directed evolution)
distractors: saturating the immune system with decoy capsids before therapeutic dosing
inserting self-peptides from MHC class I to disguise as host proteins
increasing capsid dose tenfold to overwhelm neutralizing antibody titers

facts.context: PEGylation of lipid nanoparticles (LNPs) for mRNA delivery:
facts.truth: extends circulation time but can induce anti-PEG antibodies affecting repeat dosing
distractors: directly enhances endosomal escape by destabilizing the lipid bilayer membrane
prevents all innate immune recognition by masking toll-like receptor ligands
increases cellular uptake through improved electrostatic binding to cell membranes

facts.truth: replacing murine framework regions with human sequences while preserving CDRs
distractors: removing all variable region domains and relying solely on constant region binding
adding additional murine sequences to increase overall binding affinity to targets
modifying only the Fc region glycosylation pattern to match human IgG1 profiles

facts.context: T-cell epitope deimmunization of therapeutic proteins involves:
facts.truth: identifying and mutating MHC-II binding peptides to reduce CD4+ T cell responses
distractors: adding highly immunogenic epitopes to redirect immune attention away from the drug
systematically removing all lysine residues to prevent ubiquitin-mediated degradation
only affecting B cell receptor recognition without altering T cell epitope presentation

facts.context: Immune tolerance to gene therapy transgenes can be induced by:
facts.truth: hepatocyte-specific expression which promotes regulatory T cell induction
distractors: maximizing transgene expression across all tissues using ubiquitous viral promoters
using the strongest available viral promoters to overwhelm the immune checkpoint system
systemic high-dose corticosteroid therapy maintained indefinitely throughout treatment

facts.context: Transient immunosuppression during AAV gene therapy:
facts.truth: can prevent capsid-directed T cell responses that eliminate transduced cells
distractors: is never used in clinical gene therapy trials due to unacceptable safety concerns
paradoxically enhances cytotoxic immune responses against vector-transduced hepatocytes
only suppresses humoral antibody responses without affecting cellular T cell immunity

facts.context: CpG depletion in therapeutic DNA/mRNA sequences reduces:
facts.truth: TLR9-mediated innate immune activation and inflammatory responses
distractors: overall protein expression levels by disrupting ribosomal translation efficiency
only bacterial contamination signals without any effect on mammalian immune sensing
transgene mRNA stability through altered secondary structure and codon optimization

facts.context: Modified nucleosides (pseudouridine, m1Ψ) in therapeutic mRNA:
facts.truth: reduce innate immune sensing by TLR7/8 and RIG-I while maintaining translation
distractors: dramatically increase inflammatory cytokine production through enhanced TLR3 activation
completely prevent ribosomal translation and require separate deprotection before dosing
are used exclusively in vaccine formulations and provide no benefit for protein therapies

facts.context: Complement activation by therapeutic nanoparticles can cause:
facts.truth: CARPA (complement activation-related pseudoallergy) requiring slow infusion
distractors: enhanced nanoparticle delivery to target tissues through opsonin-mediated endocytosis
no clinically significant effects because complement proteins are rapidly neutralized
reactions that only occur with recombinant protein therapeutics, never with nanoparticles
